title: Kangaroo Level 1-2 - Practice Set 19: Patterns and Counting
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: How many stars are in the picture?
questions.image_description: Four five-pointed stars drawn in a row.
questions.solution: Count each star one by one: 1, 2, 3, 4. There are 4 stars.

questions.question_text: What is 5 + 3?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='100' y='32' text-anchor='middle' font-size='24' stroke='currentColor'>5 + 3 = ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: The expression 5 + 3 = ? shown in large text.

questions.question_text: Which shape has exactly three sides?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 250 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='5' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><polygon points='65,15 85,45 45,45'/><circle cx='115' cy='30' r='15'/><polygon points='155,15 175,15 185,30 175,45 155,45 145,30'/><polygon points='215,15 235,30 215,45'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Five shapes: square, triangle, circle, hexagon, and another triangle-like shape.
questions.solution: A triangle has exactly three sides.

questions.question_text: What comes next in the pattern: circle, square, circle, square, ?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 250 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='20' cy='25' r='12'/><rect x='48' y='13' width='24' height='24'/><circle cx='100' cy='25' r='12'/><rect x='128' y='13' width='24' height='24'/><text x='190' y='32' font-size='24' stroke='currentColor'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Row showing circle, square, circle, square, then a question mark.
questions.solution: The pattern alternates circle and square. After the second square comes a circle.

questions.question_text: Mia has 7 apples and eats 2. How many apples are left?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='15' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='40' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='65' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='90' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='115' cy='30' r='10'/><line x1='130' y1='30' x2='150' y2='30'/><circle cx='165' cy='30' r='10' stroke-dasharray='3,2'/><circle cx='190' cy='30' r='10' stroke-dasharray='3,2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Five solid apples, then two dashed apples representing the eaten ones.
questions.solution: 7 - 2 = 5 apples remain.

questions.question_text: Which number is the biggest?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 250 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='125' y='32' text-anchor='middle' font-size='22' stroke='currentColor'>4   9   2   6   1</text></svg>
questions.image_description: The numbers 4, 9, 2, 6, and 1 shown in a row.
questions.solution: Among 4, 9, 2, 6, 1, the biggest is 9.

questions.question_text: The clock shows 3 o'clock. What time will it be in 1 hour?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='40'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='50' y2='20'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='72' y2='50'/><text x='50' y='15' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' stroke='currentColor'>12</text><text x='88' y='54' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' stroke='currentColor'>3</text><text x='50' y='92' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' stroke='currentColor'>6</text><text x='12' y='54' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' stroke='currentColor'>9</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Clock face showing the hour hand on 3 and the minute hand on 12.
questions.solution: One hour after 3 o'clock is 4 o'clock.

questions.question_text: How many squares are shown below?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='55' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='100' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='145' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='190' y='10' width='20' height='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Five square shapes drawn in a row.
questions.solution: Count each square: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. There are 5 squares.

questions.question_text: What number completes the equation: 12 - ? = 5?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='110' y='32' text-anchor='middle' font-size='22' stroke='currentColor'>12 - ? = 5</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Equation 12 minus a missing number equals 5.
questions.solution: 12 - 7 = 5, so the missing number is 7.

questions.question_text: In the sequence 2, 4, 6, 8, ?, what is the next number?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 250 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='125' y='32' text-anchor='middle' font-size='22' stroke='currentColor'>2,  4,  6,  8,  ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: The numbers 2, 4, 6, 8, followed by a question mark.
questions.solution: Each number increases by 2. After 8 comes 10.

questions.question_text: How many corners does a rectangle have?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 70' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='15' y='15' width='90' height='40'/><circle cx='15' cy='15' r='3'/><circle cx='105' cy='15' r='3'/><circle cx='15' cy='55' r='3'/><circle cx='105' cy='55' r='3'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Rectangle with small dots marked at each of its four corners.
questions.solution: A rectangle has 4 corners.

questions.question_text: Tom is taller than Ann. Ann is taller than Ben. Who is the shortest?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='20' y='15' width='20' height='55'/><text x='30' y='78' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' stroke='currentColor'>Tom</text><rect x='90' y='30' width='20' height='40'/><text x='100' y='78' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' stroke='currentColor'>Ann</text><rect x='160' y='45' width='20' height='25'/><text x='170' y='78' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' stroke='currentColor'>Ben</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three bars showing Tom tallest, Ann middle, Ben shortest.
questions.solution: Tom > Ann > Ben, so Ben is the shortest.

questions.question_text: How many triangles are in the figure below?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><polygon points='80,10 20,70 140,70'/><line x1='80' y1='10' x2='80' y2='70'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle divided by a vertical line down the middle, making two smaller triangles inside.
questions.solution: There are two small triangles plus the big outer triangle, giving 3 triangles.

questions.question_text: A box has 4 red balls and 6 blue balls. How many balls in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='15' cy='25' r='8'/><circle cx='35' cy='25' r='8'/><circle cx='55' cy='25' r='8'/><circle cx='75' cy='25' r='8'/><line x1='95' y1='10' x2='95' y2='40'/><circle cx='115' cy='25' r='8' stroke-dasharray='2,2'/><circle cx='135' cy='25' r='8' stroke-dasharray='2,2'/><circle cx='155' cy='25' r='8' stroke-dasharray='2,2'/><circle cx='175' cy='25' r='8' stroke-dasharray='2,2'/><circle cx='195' cy='25' r='8' stroke-dasharray='2,2'/><circle cx='215' cy='25' r='8' stroke-dasharray='2,2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Four solid circles on the left and six dashed circles on the right, separated by a vertical line.
questions.solution: 4 + 6 = 10 balls total.

questions.question_text: Which shape is missing from the pattern: triangle, circle, triangle, ?, triangle, circle?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 280 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><polygon points='15,40 30,10 45,40'/><circle cx='75' cy='25' r='12'/><polygon points='105,40 120,10 135,40'/><text x='165' y='32' font-size='22' stroke='currentColor'>?</text><polygon points='195,40 210,10 225,40'/><circle cx='255' cy='25' r='12'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Shapes triangle, circle, triangle, question mark, triangle, circle in a row.
questions.solution: The pattern alternates triangle and circle. The missing shape is a circle.

questions.question_text: Which pair of numbers adds up to 10?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='120' y='35' text-anchor='middle' font-size='18' stroke='currentColor'>Find two that make 10</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Title asking for two numbers that sum to 10.
questions.solution: 6 + 4 = 10. The other pairs give 8, 9, 9, and 9.

questions.question_text: Four kangaroos stand in a line. Red is first, Blue is just behind Red, Green is last, and Yellow is between Blue and Green. What position is Yellow?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='10' y='15' width='40' height='25'/><text x='30' y='32' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' stroke='currentColor'>Red</text><rect x='60' y='15' width='40' height='25'/><text x='80' y='32' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' stroke='currentColor'>Blue</text><rect x='110' y='15' width='40' height='25'/><text x='130' y='32' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' stroke='currentColor'>?</text><rect x='160' y='15' width='40' height='25'/><text x='180' y='32' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' stroke='currentColor'>Green</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Four slots labeled Red, Blue, question mark, and Green.
questions.solution: The line is Red, Blue, Yellow, Green. Yellow is third.

questions.question_text: Lisa had some candies, ate 3, then her mom gave her 5 more. Now she has 9. How many did she have at the start?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='130' y='30' text-anchor='middle' font-size='16' stroke='currentColor'>? - 3 + 5 = 9</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Equation ? minus 3 plus 5 equals 9.
questions.solution: Work backward: 9 - 5 + 3 = 7. She started with 7 candies.

questions.question_text: How many small squares are on the grid below?
questions.image_description: A 3 by 3 grid of small squares inside a larger square.
questions.solution: The grid is 3 rows by 3 columns, so there are 3 x 3 = 9 small squares.

questions.question_text: The pattern goes 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, ... What is the 7th number?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='130' y='30' text-anchor='middle' font-size='18' stroke='currentColor'>1, 3, 5, 7, 9, ?, ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Odd number sequence 1, 3, 5, 7, 9 with two more missing terms.

questions.question_text: How many sides does a hexagon have?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><polygon points='50,10 85,30 85,70 50,90 15,70 15,30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A regular hexagon drawn with six equal sides.
questions.solution: A hexagon has 6 sides.

questions.question_text: Three children share 12 cookies equally. How many cookies does each child get?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 70' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='40' cy='35' r='18'/><text x='40' y='39' text-anchor='middle' font-size='12' stroke='currentColor'>?</text><circle cx='120' cy='35' r='18'/><text x='120' y='39' text-anchor='middle' font-size='12' stroke='currentColor'>?</text><circle cx='200' cy='35' r='18'/><text x='200' y='39' text-anchor='middle' font-size='12' stroke='currentColor'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three circles each containing a question mark, representing three children.
questions.solution: 12 divided by 3 equals 4. Each child gets 4 cookies.

questions.question_text: Ella starts reading at 2 o'clock and finishes at 5 o'clock. How many hours did she read?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='35'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='50' y2='22'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='70' y2='50'/><text x='50' y='95' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' stroke='currentColor'>Start 2:00</text><circle cx='170' cy='50' r='35'/><line x1='170' y1='50' x2='170' y2='22'/><line x1='170' y1='50' x2='190' y2='50'/><text x='170' y='95' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' stroke='currentColor'>End 5:00</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Two clocks, the first at 2 o'clock labeled Start, the second at 5 o'clock labeled End.
questions.solution: From 2 o'clock to 5 o'clock is 3 hours.

questions.question_text: A kangaroo jumps 2 meters each hop. How many hops to travel 10 meters?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><line x1='10' y1='45' x2='250' y2='45'/><path d='M 20 45 Q 40 15 60 45' /><path d='M 60 45 Q 80 15 100 45' /><path d='M 100 45 Q 120 15 140 45' /><path d='M 140 45 Q 160 15 180 45' /><path d='M 180 45 Q 200 15 220 45' /><text x='20' y='58' text-anchor='middle' font-size='9' stroke='currentColor'>0</text><text x='220' y='58' text-anchor='middle' font-size='9' stroke='currentColor'>10m</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A number line from 0 to 10 meters with arcs showing hops of 2 meters each.
questions.solution: 10 divided by 2 equals 5. The kangaroo needs 5 hops.
